name: Cloud Service Stack
summary: A simple way to map whether a workload really needs raw infrastructure, a managed platform, software, or a data cloud.
whenToUseIt: Use it when a cloud conversation feels abstract or overloaded with vendor language.
steps: Define the actual job to be done
Place that job on the stack: infrastructure, platform, software, or data
Identify which responsibilities stay with your team
Check whether the chosen layer is too low-level or too constrained
example: A team that mainly needs application hosting may be better served by a platform approach like Heroku than by jumping straight into low-level infrastructure choices.
